name: Pingarrón Hills Bicycle Loop from San Martín de la Vega
name_original: Parque Regional del Sureste – circular por Vallequillas desde San Martín de la Vega – gravel
description: This moderate 17.9-mile touring cycling route in Parque Regional del Sureste offers mostly paved surfaces and takes about 2 hours 34 minutes
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega are characterized by a diverse landscape within the Community of Madrid. The area features the meandering Jarama River, creating fertile alluvial plains and riverine environments, alongside gently rolling hills, gypsum crests, and pine forests. Situated within the Sureste Regional Park, the region includes natural reserves like the Campillo Lagoon, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Many routes combine paved surfaces with unpaved segments, catering to different touring cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around San Martín De La Vega The most popular touring cycling route is Pingarrón Hills Bicycle Loop from San Martín de la Vega, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route features undulating terrain with around 250m of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Toledo Bridge – San Fermín Pathway loop from San Martín de la Vega, a moderate 36.1 miles (58.1 km) path. This longer route follows riverine landscapes and offers views across the plains. Local touring cyclists also love the La Marañosa – View of the Madrid Skyline loop from San Martín de la Vega, a 20.1 miles (32.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Touring cycling around San Martín De La Vega is defined by the Jarama River's plains, rolling hills, and segments within the Sureste Regional Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more challenging routes. The routes in San Martín De La Vega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore San Martín De La Vega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around San Martín de la Vega?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes in the San Martín de la Vega area, offering a wide range of experiences for cyclists. These routes cater to various skill levels, from leisurely rides along river plains to more challenging tours through rolling hills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in San Martín de la Vega?
A: The terrain around San Martín de la Vega is quite diverse. You'll find routes along the meandering Jarama River, offering relatively flat and scenic paths. Other areas feature gently rolling hills, gypsum crests, and pine forests, providing varied visual interest and moderate elevation changes. Many routes combine paved surfaces with unpaved segments.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in San Martín de la Vega?
A: Yes, San Martín de la Vega offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. In total, there are 6 easy routes available. These typically follow flatter sections, often along the river, providing a pleasant introduction to the area's landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in San Martín de la Vega?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, routes with lower elevation gain and mostly paved surfaces are generally suitable. Consider routes that explore the riverine landscapes, which tend to be flatter. Always check the route details on komoot for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What are some notable natural features or attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle along the Jarama River, explore parts of the Sureste Regional Park, and visit significant natural reserves like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2572064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, known for waterfowl. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miralrío Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2667434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Hide at San Juan Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, some routes pass by points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4980472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Vértice Geodésico Campillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in San Martín de la Vega are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toledo Bridge – San Fermín Pathway loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 58.1 km ride, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10412849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciempozuelos – Cerro del Portillo loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 32.3 km route with moderate difficulty.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in San Martín de la Vega?
A: The best seasons for touring cycling in San Martín de la Vega are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable rides. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures.

Q: How do other touring cyclists rate the routes in San Martín de la Vega?
A: The touring cycling routes in San Martín de la Vega are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of paved and unpaved sections, and the opportunities to explore both riverine environments and rolling hills.

Q: Can I reach San Martín de la Vega by public transport with my bike?
A: San Martín de la Vega's proximity to Madrid suggests good accessibility. While specific rules for bikes on public transport (trains, buses) can vary, many regional transport services in Spain accommodate bicycles, especially outside of peak hours. It's advisable to check with local transport providers for their current policies regarding bikes.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 11 difficult routes available. These routes typically feature more significant elevation gains and may include longer distances or more demanding terrain. An example of a moderate route with notable elevation is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38268541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciempozuelos – Camino to San Martín de la Vega loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 28.3 km with 150m of ascent.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Madrid skyline?
A: Yes, some routes in the area provide unique vantage points. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38273996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marañosa – View of the Madrid Skyline loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 32.3 km route that leads through varied landscapes and offers glimpses of the Madrid skyline, combining natural beauty with urban views.
tips: This river, the longest in the Community of Madrid, is in turn one of the main tributaries of the Tagus River. Its channel constitutes the only biological corridor that runs through the region from north to south, in a delicate balance between the massively urbanized and industrialized areas that surround it.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 28.8 kilometers long and generally takes about 2 hours and 34 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Vallequillas circular route?
faqs.answer: Despite the 'gravel' in its name, this route features mostly paved surfaces. While there might be some unpaved sections, the majority of the 28.8-kilometer journey is on asphalt or similar paved material, making for a relatively smooth ride. Any unpaved portions are generally manageable for a touring bike.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, while classified as a 'moderate' bike ride requiring 'good fitness' due to its length and elevation gain, the predominantly paved surfaces make it accessible for a wide range of cyclists, including beginners and families. The unpaved sections are likely not overly technical.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling the Parque Regional del Sureste – circular by Vallequillas from San Martín de la Vega – gravel?
faqs.answer: The route starts from San Martín de la Vega. You should be able to find parking within the town, though specific parking areas for trail access are not detailed. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Parque Regional del Sureste?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route within the Parque Regional del Sureste. It is a public natural area, and access for cycling is generally free.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Parque Regional del Sureste is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs on this particular cycling route are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Please ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling alongside you for this distance.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and interesting natural landscapes within the Parque Regional del Sureste. The area is characterized by the low courses of the Manzanares and Jarama rivers, offering fluvial groves, lagoons, and unique gypsum cliffs. You'll also pass through agricultural fields and steppes, with opportunities to observe diverse flora and fauna, particularly waterfowl.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or landmarks on this route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can experience several notable points of interest. These include a beautiful Jarama River view, the historic San Martín Bridge (Spanish Civil War), and the Memorial to Kit Conway and the Fallen of the International Brigades.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Parque Regional del Sureste can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, and winters can be chilly, though often sunny.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Ruta Verde 2.2 Canal del Jarama, MTB Madrid a Aranjuez, Camino viejo de Madrid a Chinchón, Camino de la Raya Alta, and Cañada Real Galiana, among others.

faqs.question: What public transport options are available to reach the trailhead in San Martín de la Vega?
faqs.answer: San Martín de la Vega is accessible by public transport from Madrid. You can typically take a bus service from Madrid to reach the town, which serves as the starting point for this route. It's recommended to check current bus schedules and routes for the most up-to-date information.
